rationale: The citing paper uses this source for a central conceptual claim that hedonic and utilitarian attitudes are distinct dimensions rather than opposite ends of one continuum. It also explicitly adopts Voss et al.'s semantic differential items to measure perceived utilitarian and hedonic benefits in the replication studies. Those measures are key mediators in the paper's main model and hypothesis tests. This is therefore more than background mention and functions as core conceptual and measurement support.
relation_description: The citing paper relies on Voss et al. (2003) to define the hedonic-versus-utilitarian attitude structure and directly borrows its measurement items for mediator operationalization. That conceptualization and scale provide a foundation for the paper's main mediation results.
